title: stemonoporus nitidus subsp. lancifolius
intro: Stemonoporus nitidus subsp. lancifolius, also known as the Lancet-leaved Stemonoporus, is a subspecies of tree in the Stemonuraceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bark of the Lancet-leaved Stemonoporus is known to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin infections. In herbal preparations, the bark can be made into a decoction or infusion, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties.
